summary: Angry shareholders confronted Nidec Corp.’s board at the motor manufacturer’s annual meeting, following a tumultuous year marred by accounting scandals, manufacturing defects and the ouster of its charismatic founder.

summary: Kevin Warsh’s affection for concision makes it much easier to report the central bank’s views in full.

title: Stocks Rise, Oil Slides After Trump Inks Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
summary: US stocks rebounded and oil prices fell as some energy shipments began to transit the Strait of Hormuz, hours after President Donald Trump signed a preliminary deal to end the war in the Middle East.

title: US Gasoline Prices Fall Below $4 as Iran Pressure Eases
summary: US gasoline prices dipped below $4 a gallon for the first time since March, providing welcome relief to consumers after a historic global supply disruption sent fuel costs soaring for months.

title: Bank of England Keeps Key Interest Rate at 3.75%
Big Tech’s AI Spending Spree Is Making Stock Buybacks Disappear
summary: The artificial-intelligence race is becoming so expensive that it’s snuffing out one of the key forces that has helped keep Big Tech stocks soaring for years: steady share buybacks.

title: Latvia Premier Calls for EU Envoy in Peace Talks With Russia
summary: The European Union needs to appoint an envoy for peace talks with Russia when the time is right, Latvian Prime Minister Andris Kulbergs said, as the bloc seeks to play a role in ending the war in Ukraine.

title: US Treasuries Rebound After Warsh’s Debut at Fed Stoked Selloff
summary: US Treasuries recovered from a selloff after the new Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh positioned himself as a credible figure to fight inflation.

title: Indonesia Taps Insider to Revive World’s Worst Stock Market
summary: Indonesia named capital markets veteran Jeffrey Hendrik as chief executive of its stock exchange, the latest effort to bolster investor confidence in the world’s worst-performing equity market.

title: SNB Keeps Up War Footing on Franc But Evolves Wording on Threat
summary: The Swiss National Bank warned investors that a Middle East peace deal hasn’t altered its state of readiness to sell the franc if such a stance is required.

title: Resonac CEO on Strong AI Demand, China Risk
summary: Resonac President and CEO Hidehito Takahashi speaks exclusively on AI demand with Bloomberg TV's Shery Ahn at the Bloomberg Look Ahead 2026 event in Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bond Traders Get a Clear Message From New Fed Chief on Inflation
summary: Warsh says central bank won’t tolerate high inflation.

title: Gold Steady as US-Iran Peace Deal Optimism Counters Hawkish Fed
summary: Gold steadied, with support stemming from the signing of an interim peace deal between the US and Iran offset by hawkish comments from the new head of the Federal Reserve.

title: First Saudi Supertankers Start Leaving Hormuz After Deal
summary: Three Saudi supertankers were among a growing list of vessels exiting the Strait of Hormuz in an early sign of rising flows after the US and Iran’s interim peace deal, although some trade groups warned that their members needed more clarity on mines and other practicalities.

title: Qatar Brings Empty LNG Ship Back for First Time Since War Began
summary: Qatar brought an empty liquefied natural gas tanker back into the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz for the first time since the war in Iran began, a sign that the key producer is preparing to ramp up exports.

title: Czechs Mull First Rate Hike Since 2022 to Curb Price Risks
summary: The Czech central bank may raise interest rates for the first time in four years as policymakers weigh domestic inflation pressures against receding risks from global energy prices.

title: Warsh's Debut Sparks Surge in Hike Bets
summary: Traders piled into betting on interest-rate hikes as soon as next month after Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh clear the central bank won’t tolerate high inflation. The hawkish message was driven home by the projections of individual Fed members. Bloomberg's Alister Bull reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Copper Falls After Fed Chairman Warsh Positions as Inflation Hawk
summary: Copper fell more than 1% to wipe out the gains of earlier this week after US Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh sparked speculation about rate hikes.

title: Oil Falls as Iran Peace Deal Gets Tankers Moving Through Hormuz
summary: Oil fell and gasoline sank after the US and Iran agreed to settle a months-long conflict that triggered the biggest supply disruption in history, and some vessels were seen making their first crossings of the Strait of Hormuz since the war began.

title: East Asian Refiners Eye Fuel Sales Boost Before Hormuz Reopen
summary: Refineries in East Asia are starting to ramp up fuel exports after months of holding back and prioritizing local sales, in what appears to be an attempt to front-run the market before the Strait of Hormuz reopens.

title: Latvian PM says EU Needs Ukraine Peace Talks Envoy
summary: “Europe has to be at the table,” Kulbergs said in an interview with Bloomberg TV on Thursday in Brussels. “There should be one person if the peace talks are in place who has the political mandate to do that.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Intel Shares Soar After Trump Says It Struck Apple Chip Deal
summary: Intel Corp.’s stock surged more than 9% in premarket trading Thursday after US President Donald Trump said the chipmaker will work alongside Apple Inc. to design and produce semiconductors domestically.

title: Fed’s Warsh Rocks Bond Market in Debut, Sparks Surge in Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Traders piled into betting on interest-rate hikes as soon as next month after Kevin Warsh used his debut press conference as Federal Reserve chairman to make clear the central bank won’t tolerate high inflation.

title: Hong Kong to Launch China Bond Futures to Promote Yuan
summary: Hong Kong is set to launch China government bond futures amid Beijing’s efforts to promote global yuan usage and attract foreign capital.

title: Hamptons Golf Tickets At $400 Look Like A Steal After the Knicks
summary: With Knicks NBA Finals seats and World Cup tickets costing thousands, golf's US Open in the Hamptons is emerging as a rare bargain. Paulina Cachero breaks down the numbers (Source: Bloomberg)

title: EQT Buys UK’s Intertek for £9.3 Billion in Latest LSE Exit
summary: Intertek Group Plc agreed to a £9.3 billion ($12.3 billion) takeover bid from private equity firm EQT AB, a deal that will mean another British company leaves the London stock market.

title: Goldman’s Kaplan Says Fed May Need to Hike as Soon as September
summary: The Federal Reserve may need to raise interest rates as soon as September if inflation remains elevated, according to Rob Kaplan, vice chairman at Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and former Dallas Fed president.

title: Atomic Lab Sounds Alarm on Screwworm Spread Across the Americas
summary: A key international nuclear lab helping to eradicate the New World screwworm says more sterile flies and better coordination with southern neighbors is needed to prevent the deadly parasite from spreading across North America.

title: Second Iran-Bound Corn Ship Crossed Hormuz Before Peace Deal
summary: A second ship laden with corn bound for Iran transited the Strait of Hormuz just hours before the US-Iran interim deal to end the war and reopen the waterway was announced.

title: Citi Sees $80 Billion Flows to India After RBI Capital Push
summary: India could attract as much as $80 billion in foreign capital by year-end after the central bank unveiled measures to make the economy more attractive to overseas citizens and investors, according to Citigroup Inc.’s top banker in the country.

title: JPMorgan Warns Chip Stock Rally Poses Risk of Market ‘Tantrums’
summary: The risk of market “tantrums” is rising as sharp swings in semiconductor stocks force some investors to cut allocations, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co. strategists.

title: China Stock Gauge Nears Bear Market on Weak Growth, Tech Slide
summary: A major Chinese stock gauge is on the cusp of a bear market, underscoring deepening concerns about the country’s growth outlook and the underperformance of its heavily weighted Internet and consumer firms.
